title: 第 113 節
en: — recognises the Logos in the patriarchs, 413, 439.
— indistinct on the soul and God, -- his psychology, 418, 419, 420.
— his bridge from earth to heaven, — eschatological language of, 425.
— allegorised, the, Old Testament, — the Logos as intervening between the Divine and matter, 455.
— treatise, De Vita Contempluiiva, ascribed to, 464.
— begins with doubting the evidence of the senses, 102.
Phraortes, from Greek Pravarti, Physical impossibilities not accepted as miracles by Philo, Clement, or Origen, 376.
—religion, 89, 90, 106, Physical Religion, importance of the Veda for, 95.
